sourceMovie.title: Splice
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Splice (2010) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Vincenzo Natali's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Fly
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Splice clicked for you, The Fly (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Splice?
faq.answer: Try The Fly, Alien: Romulus, Eraserhead—each shares DNA with Splice while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Splice?
faq.answer: Splice (2010) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Splice worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
